title: Sardinian Pork Amatriciana Spaghetti
description: Fatty, crispy Italian dish featuring brine-wet Sardinian pork and keto-friendly unsalted butter. Perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Cook the spaghetti in a large pot of boiling, salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium-high heat. Add the sliced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until caramelized and golden brown.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the guanciale or pancetta to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until crispy.
Add the canned tomatoes, red pepper flakes, and salt to the skillet. Stir to combine.
Add the cooked spaghetti to the skillet, tossing to combine with the tomato sauce.
Add the reserved pasta water to the skillet, if needed, to achieve a saucy consistency.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the skillet from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh basil and grated Parmesan cheese.
Cook the brine-wet Sardinian pork shoulder in a separate oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat until browned on all sides.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the pork is cooked through and crispy on the outside.
Slice the cooked pork and serve alongside the amatriciana spaghetti.
